architecture.summary: A neuro-symbolic ILP framework built on differentiable Neural Logic networks that jointly learns first-order logic rules and non-linear functions over mixed discrete-continuous domains. The system uses neural components to learn continuous transformations while maintaining logical rule structure for interpretability, combining deep learning's function approximation capacity with ILP's relational reasoning and data efficiency.

biblio.title: Learning explanatory logical rules in non-linear domains: a neuro-symbolic approach

claims.statement: Architecture's capability to identify non-linear functions from continuous data
Neuro-symbolic ILP framework is tailored for non-linear rule extraction in mixed discrete-continuous spaces
Methodology synergises deep learning with relational ILP models to enhance interpretability and create logical decision boundaries
ILP grapples with challenges, notably the handling of non-linearity in continuous domains
